Functions for button initialization and state checking in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides an interface for managing button states. It includes the necessary header files `button_id.h`, `stdbool.h`, and `stdint.h` to support button identification and standard data types. The function [`button_init`](<#button_init>) initializes the button system. The function [`button_is_pressed`](<#button_is_pressed>) checks if a button, identified by `ButtonId`, is currently pressed and returns a boolean value. The function [`button_get_state_bits`](<#button_get_state_bits>) returns an 8-bit unsigned integer representing the state of all buttons as a bitmask.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `button_id.h`
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
